gap_messages.request_logged: describe the exact replay request that was executed
gap_messages.response_logged: describe the exploit response that was observed
gap_messages.payload_observed: link the payload to the observed output
gap_messages.target_bound: bind the PoC to the same target/endpoint as the finding
gap_messages.runtime_command_logged: include the exact replay command that was executed
gap_messages.runtime_response_logged: include runtime response details (status/body) for that replay
gap_messages.runtime_http: include HTTP status evidence from runtime replay
gap_messages.runtime_signal: include impact evidence from runtime replay
gap_messages.runtime_impact: show runtime impact (data/error/access change), not only heartbeat output
gap_messages.runtime_host_bound: use the same PoC host in runtime replay evidence
gap_messages.runtime_payload_bound: use the same PoC payload in runtime replay evidence

hypothesis_rules.statement_template: Reachable endpoint {entity} may expose actionable attack surface.
Exposed service on {entity}:{value} should be tested for service-level abuse.
Detected technology {entity} {value} may contain version-specific weaknesses.
Vulnerability signal from {source_tool} against {entity} should be replay-validated.

tool_alternatives.nmap: [BLOCKED BY FW / SLOW] Nmap failed. SUGGESTION: 1) Try `naabu -top-ports 1000 -c 50`. 2) If still blocked, use `masscan` or perform highly targeted TCP SYN via custom Scapy script.
tool_alternatives.sqlmap: [WAF / TIME-BLIND] SQLmap failed. SUGGESTION: 1) Switch to `ghauri` for advanced evasion. 2) Fallback to `curl` with manual time-delay payloads (e.g., `pg_sleep(10)`). 3) Test for NoSQLi instead (`nosqli`).
tool_alternatives.subfinder: [API LIMITS] Subfinder exhausted. SUGGESTION: 1) Correlate with `amass enum -passive`. 2) Use `shosubgo` or `github-subdomains` for OSINT discovery.
tool_alternatives.ffuf: [WAF BAN / 403s] Ffuf getting 403s. SUGGESTION: 1) Add header `-H 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1'`. 2) Try path normalization evasion (`/%2e/`). 3) Use `feroxbuster --random-agent`.
tool_alternatives.httpx: [TIMEOUTS] Httpx hanging. SUGGESTION: Run with `-rl 50 -t 50 -retries 2` to throttle requests and prevent WAF blacklisting.
tool_alternatives.nuclei: [NO CVE FOUND] Nuclei finished with no results. SUGGESTION: STOP relying on templates. Transition immediately to Phase 5: Manual Business Logic testing. Open Python and write a script to test IDORs on API endpoints.
tool_alternatives.katana: [JS RENDER FAILED] Katana cannot see links. SUGGESTION: The target is a modern SPA (React/Vue). Katana won't work well alone. Use a Puppeteer script or manually inspect the main `.js` bundle using `linkfinder` or `jsluice`.
tool_alternatives.arjun: Try x8 or param-miner for parameter discovery
tool_alternatives.jwt_tool: Use Burp Suite's JWT extension if intercepting
tool_alternatives.wpscan: [API REQUIRED] WPScan limited. SUGGESTION: Use `nuclei -t http/wordpress/` or manually inspect `/wp-content/plugins/` directory.
tool_alternatives.wfuzz: Migrate to ffuf or feroxbuster for better performance in Go/Rust
tool_alternatives.trufflehog: [FALSE POSITIVES] Trufflehog overwhelming. SUGGESTION: Use `gitleaks detect` or manually run `ripgrep` for specific patterns (e.g., `rg -i 'api_key|secret|password'` in `.js` files).
tool_alternatives.dalfox: [BLIND XSS] Dalfox failed. SUGGESTION: Use `interactsh-client` and manually inject payload `<script>fetch('http://[id].interact.sh')</script>` into every parameter and header via `var_mapper.py`.
tool_alternatives.browser_action: [DOM INTERACTION] Browser automation failed. SUGGESTION: Don't give up. The application might require a specific sequence of clicks or login state. Write a targeted Python Playwright/Puppeteer script to interact with the DOM slowly.

recon_phase_hints.live_host_validation: CRITICAL: After subdomain enumeration, ALWAYS validate which hosts are alive before port scanning or directory brute-force. Use host probing tools to filter dead/unresolved hosts. Never scan dead hosts.

analysis_phase_hints: Compare authenticated vs unauthenticated responses — missing access controls often show here
HTTP method override: check for _method, X-HTTP-Method-Override, X-Method-Override headers
Mass assignment: submit extra parameters that should be admin-only (role=admin, is_admin=true, privilege_level=9)
Cache poisoning: modify X-Forwarded-For, Origin, Host headers to poison cached responses
Parameter pollution: submit duplicate parameters (a=safe&a=dangerous) to confuse validators
JWT claims manipulation: if JWT visible, inspect claims in base64 — modify exp, role, user_id offline
CORS misconfiguration: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflects your origin or is wildcard
Content-Type confusion: change Content-Type to application/json while sending form data or vice versa
Data type confusion: submit strings where integers expected (e.g., amount=1e5 instead of 100000)
Business logic flaws: race conditions in transfers, order/payment confirmations, or approval workflows
Session fixation: check if Set-Cookie lacks secure/HttpOnly/SameSite flags
Account enumeration: profile registration/password-reset responses for timing or content differences
Insecure deserialization: if app accepts serialized objects (PHP, Java, Python pickle), probe for gadgets
IDOR via pattern: increment numeric IDs (user_id=1, 2, 3) or test predictable identifiers (usernames)
Template injection: if user input reaches template engines, test {{ }}, <% %>, ${} syntax for execution

specialist_prompts.sqli: Focus EXCLUSIVELY on SQL injection. Test all input parameters for error-based, blind time-based, and UNION-based SQLi. Use manual payloads first, then confirm with automated tooling.
specialist_prompts.xss: Focus EXCLUSIVELY on Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). Test all input reflection points for stored, reflected, and DOM-based XSS. Use manual confirmation first, then automated scanning to verify findings.
specialist_prompts.ssrf: Focus EXCLUSIVELY on SSRF. Test all URL/redirect/callback parameters. Try AWS metadata (169.254.169.254), internal hosts (127.0.0.1, localhost), and protocol wrappers (file://, gopher://, dict://).
specialist_prompts.lfi: Focus EXCLUSIVELY on LFI and Path Traversal. Test all file/path/include parameters with traversal sequences (../), null bytes, and encoding variants.
specialist_prompts.recon: Perform deep reconnaissance ONLY. Enumerate subdomains, open ports, directories, and JavaScript files. Map all endpoints and parameters. Do NOT attempt exploitation.
specialist_prompts.exploit: Test and exploit all discovered input parameters. Use all available fuzzing and scanning tools. Focus on achieving demonstrable impact.
specialist_prompts.analyzer: Focus EXCLUSIVELY on source code and configuration analysis. Use code_analysis tool to run static analysis scans. Review application logic, authentication flows, and data handling patterns. Look for hardcoded secrets, insecure crypto, and logic flaws.
specialist_prompts.reporter: Focus EXCLUSIVELY on generating comprehensive vulnerability reports. Review all findings from the session and create detailed create_vulnerability_report entries for each confirmed issue. Include proper CVSS scores, PoC scripts, and remediation steps.

tool_descriptions.nmap: Network scanner — ports, services, OS, vulnerabilities (-sV, -O, --script vuln)
tool_descriptions.masscan: Fast TCP port scanner — all ports in minutes
tool_descriptions.rustscan: Modern port scanner — pipe to nmap for service detection
tool_descriptions.httpx: HTTP probe — check live web servers, status codes, tech detection
tool_descriptions.assetfinder: Find domains and subdomains related to a target
tool_descriptions.nuclei: Template-based vulnerability scanner — fast targeted tests
tool_descriptions.feroxbuster: Fast content discovery for web servers
tool_descriptions.katana: Crawler — discover all endpoints, JS files, forms
tool_descriptions.waybackurls: Fetch URLs from wayback machine for domain
tool_descriptions.gau: GetAllUrls — fetch URLs from multiple sources (wayback, commoncrawl, OTX)
tool_descriptions.hakrawler: Web crawler — find endpoints, JS, parameters
tool_descriptions.dalfox: XSS scanner — fast, targeted XSS testing
tool_descriptions.arjun: HTTP parameter discovery — find hidden parameters
tool_descriptions.kiterunner: API endpoint discovery with content routing
tool_descriptions.massdns: Fast DNS resolver for subdomain validation
tool_descriptions.gospider: Web spider — crawl and discover endpoints
tool_descriptions.waymore: Wayback machine URL discovery with filtering
tool_descriptions.zaproxy: OWASP ZAP — web application security scanner
tool_descriptions.cmseek: CMS detection and exploitation (WordPress, Joomla, Drupal)
tool_descriptions.xsstrike: Advanced XSS detection with context analysis
tool_descriptions.chisel: HTTP tunnel — port forwarding through web proxies
tool_descriptions.socat: Bidirectional data relay — port forwarding, reverse shells
tool_descriptions.responder: LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS responder — credential capture on LAN
tool_descriptions.hydra: Login brute force — HTTP, SSH, FTP, SMB
tool_descriptions.medusa: Login brute force — alternative to hydra
tool_descriptions.john: John the Ripper — password hash cracking
tool_descriptions.linkfinder: Find endpoints and parameters in JavaScript files
tool_descriptions.js-scan: Analyze JavaScript for secrets, endpoints, APIs
tool_descriptions.jsfinder: Find URLs and secrets in JS files
tool_descriptions.linksdumper: Extract links from JavaScript at scale
tool_descriptions.semgrep: Static code analysis — find vulnerability patterns
tool_descriptions.grep: Search files for patterns — secrets, endpoints, parameters
tool_descriptions.eslint: JavaScript linter — can detect security issues
tool_descriptions.cut-cdn: Remove CDN IPs from IP lists
tool_descriptions.vulnx: CMS vulnerability scanner with automatic exploitation
tool_descriptions.fuzzdb: Fuzzing database of payloads and wordlists
tool_descriptions.pspy64: Process snooping — detect scheduled tasks and cron jobs
tool_descriptions.kxss: XSS scanner — find reflected XSS in URL parameters
tool_descriptions.sstimap: Server-side template injection detection and exploitation
tool_descriptions.bypass-firewalls-by-DNS-history: Bypass WAF by resolving IP from DNS history
tool_descriptions.param-miner: Parameter mining — find hidden query parameters
tool_descriptions.hakip2host: Convert IP range to hostname list
tool_descriptions.testssl: SSL/TLS protocol testing and cipher analysis
tool_descriptions.interactsh-client: Out-of-band interaction testing (OAST) for blind vulns
tool_descriptions.python3: Custom scripting for PoC, automation, and complex tests
tool_descriptions.bash: Shell scripting for chaining tools and automating workflows
tool_descriptions.curl: HTTP client — flexible request building and API testing
tool_descriptions.meg: Mass HTTP fetcher — parallel request handling for many URLs/paths
tool_descriptions.qsreplace: Replace query string parameters — useful for payload injection
tool_descriptions.dsieve: Filter and deduplicate lists of domains/IPs

ctf.crypto: CTF cryptography challenges — RSA attacks, AES weaknesses, padding oracle, hash cracking, XOR, elliptic curves, and classical ciphers with Python pycryptodome and CLI tools
ctf.forensics: CTF forensics — file carving, steganography, pcap/network analysis, memory forensics, disk forensics, and metadata extraction using CLI tools in Kali Linux
ctf.pwn: CTF binary exploitation — buffer overflow, format string, ROP chains, ret2libc, heap exploitation, shellcode, using pwntools and GDB with PEDA/pwndbg in Docker Kali Linux
ctf.reversing: CTF reverse engineering — static analysis with radare2/objdump, dynamic analysis with GDB/ltrace/strace, anti-debug bypass, patching, and decompilation without GUI tools

frameworks.django: Security testing playbook for Django applications covering debug mode, admin exposure, ORM injection, CSRF, SSTI, and Django-specific misconfigurations
frameworks.dotnet: Security testing playbook for ASP.NET / .NET Core applications covering ViewState deserialization, Razor SSTI, NTLM auth bypass, IIS misconfigurations, and .NET-specific attack techniques
frameworks.express: Security testing playbook for Express.js/Node.js applications covering prototype pollution, SSRF, regex DoS, JWT misuse, path traversal, and Node.js-specific misconfigurations
frameworks.fastapi: Security testing playbook for FastAPI applications covering ASGI, dependency injection, and API vulnerabilities
frameworks.flask: Security testing playbook for Flask applications covering Werkzeug debugger RCE, SSTI via Jinja2, session forgery, misconfigurations, and Flask-specific attack patterns
frameworks.laravel: Security testing playbook for Laravel applications covering debug mode, route enumeration, mass assignment, deserialization, and Laravel-specific misconfigurations
frameworks.nextjs: Security testing playbook for Next.js covering App Router, Server Actions, RSC, and Edge runtime vulnerabilities
frameworks.php: Security testing playbook for generic PHP applications covering type juggling, file inclusion, deserialization, phpinfo exposure, eval injection, and PHP-specific misconfigurations
frameworks.rails: Security testing playbook for Ruby on Rails applications covering mass assignment, CSRF, route enumeration, deserialization, and Rails-specific misconfigurations
frameworks.spring: Security testing playbook for Spring Boot/MVC applications covering Actuator endpoints, SSTI via Thymeleaf, Spring4Shell, EL injection, and Java deserialization
frameworks.wordpress: Security testing playbook for WordPress covering user enumeration, xmlrpc abuse, plugin/theme vulnerabilities, wp-admin brute force, and wpscan methodology

payloads.sqli: Full SQL Injection Payload Library (400+ payloads)
payloads.ssti: Full SSTI (Server-Side Template Injection) Payload Library

postexploit.lateral_movement: Lateral movement techniques — pass-the-hash, pass-the-ticket, psexec, wmiexec, evil-winrm, crackmapexec spray, and moving through Windows/Linux networks after initial access
postexploit.linux_privesc: Linux privilege escalation — SUID/SGID abuse, sudo misconfigurations, writable cron jobs, capabilities, PATH hijacking, kernel exploits, and linpeas automation
postexploit.pivoting: Network pivoting and tunneling — chisel SOCKS proxy, ligolo-ng, socat port forwarding, SSH tunneling, proxychains, and reaching internal networks through a compromised host
postexploit.windows_privesc: Windows privilege escalation — AlwaysInstallElevated, unquoted service paths, weak service permissions, token impersonation, SeImpersonatePrivilege, winpeas, and credential dumping

protocols.active_directory: Active Directory attack techniques covering enumeration, Kerberoasting, AS-REP Roasting, Pass-the-Hash, DCSync, ADCS ESC attacks, and ACL abuse
protocols.dns: DNS security testing — zone transfer, subdomain enumeration, DNS cache poisoning, DNS tunneling detection, DNSSEC bypass, and DNS-based information disclosure
protocols.ftp: FTP security testing — anonymous login, brute force, bounce attacks, FTPS misconfiguration, vsftpd backdoor CVE, and sensitive file extraction
protocols.graphql: GraphQL security testing covering introspection, resolver injection, batching attacks, and authorization bypass
protocols.kerberos: Kerberos attack techniques — AS-REP Roasting, Kerberoasting, Pass-the-Ticket, Golden/Silver Ticket, Overpass-the-Hash using impacket and kerbrute in Kali Linux
protocols.ldap: LDAP security testing covering injection, anonymous bind, enumeration, LDAP-based auth bypass, and Active Directory LDAP attacks
protocols.rdp: RDP security testing — BlueKeep CVE-2019-0708, DejaBlue, credential brute force, NLA bypass, session hijacking, and RDP-specific misconfiguration testing
protocols.smb: SMB/NetBIOS security testing — null session, share enumeration, EternalBlue, pass-the-hash, relay attacks, crackmapexec, smbclient, and SMB-specific CVEs
protocols.smtp_imap: SMTP and IMAP security testing covering open relay, email header injection, user enumeration, credential brute force, and SMTP as SSRF pivot
protocols.snmp: SNMP security testing — community string enumeration, MIB walking, SNMP v1/v2c/v3 brute force, information disclosure, and device configuration extraction
protocols.ssh: SSH security testing covering user enumeration, brute force, key analysis, tunneling for pivoting, known CVEs, and SSH-specific misconfiguration testing

reconnaissance.asn_whois_osint: ASN/CIDR discovery, WHOIS lookups, BGP enumeration, IP range mapping, and OSINT passive reconnaissance to map the full attack surface of an organization without active scanning
reconnaissance.ctf_methodology: CTF Challenge Methodology — Thinking + Execution Framework
reconnaissance.dorking: Google dorking and OSINT search techniques for passive reconnaissance — find exposed panels, leaked credentials, sensitive files, and attack surface using web_search without API keys
reconnaissance.exposed_devtools_detection: Detect and assess publicly exposed development tools including Storybook component libraries, serial terminals, hardware debug interfaces, admin panels on dev clusters, and developer-only services that should never be internet-facing
reconnaissance.internal_pentest: Local & Internal Penetration Testing — Methodology Framework
reconnaissance.js_internal_hostname_intelligence: Deep JavaScript bundle analysis focused on extracting internal hostnames, fleet/cluster naming conventions, obfuscated infrastructure references, and non-API internal service URLs that reveal backend topology
reconnaissance.monitoring_secrets_exposure: Detect and exploit exposed monitoring/observability credentials including Sentry DSN, OpenTelemetry keys, Datadog API keys, Honeycomb tokens, and similar secrets in JS bundles and HTTP responses

technologies.cloud_security: Cloud security assessment for AWS, GCP, and Azure covering credential abuse, IAM privilege escalation, storage misconfigs, and serverless attacks
technologies.docker_container: Security testing playbook for Docker and container environments covering container escape, privileged containers, exposed Docker API, misconfigurations, and Kubernetes enumeration
technologies.elasticsearch: Security testing playbook for Elasticsearch covering unauthenticated access, data extraction, index enumeration, and Kibana security misconfigurations
technologies.firebase_firestore: Firebase/Firestore security testing covering security rules, Cloud Functions, and client-side trust issues
technologies.gitlab_github: Security testing playbook for GitLab and GitHub Enterprise covering exposed repositories, CI/CD pipeline injection, token extraction, IDOR, and self-hosted instance vulnerabilities
technologies.jenkins: Security testing playbook for Jenkins CI/CD covering unauthenticated access, Script Console RCE, Groovy injection, job configuration abuse, credential extraction, and known CVEs
technologies.memcached: Security testing playbook for Memcached covering unauthenticated access, data extraction, cache poisoning, SSRF-to-Memcached, and UDP reflection amplification
technologies.mobile_app_pentesting: Mobile application security testing for Android APK and iOS IPA in headless Docker environments, covering static analysis, constrained dynamic testing, and evidence-focused reporting.
technologies.mongodb: Security testing playbook for MongoDB covering unauthenticated access, NoSQL injection, data extraction, and MongoDB-specific attack techniques
technologies.nginx_apache: Security testing playbook for Nginx and Apache web servers covering misconfigurations, path traversal, alias bypass, server-side includes, and common CVEs
technologies.observability_stack_attacks: Attack techniques against exposed observability infrastructure including OpenTelemetry collectors, Prometheus /metrics, Grafana, Jaeger, Zipkin, and similar monitoring backends — including credential brute-force, data injection, and information extraction
technologies.redis: Security testing playbook for Redis covering unauthenticated access, RCE via cron/SSH key injection, SSRF-to-Redis, Lua scripting, and data extraction
technologies.supabase: Supabase security testing covering Row Level Security, PostgREST, Edge Functions, and service key exposure
technologies.tomcat: Security testing playbook for Apache Tomcat covering manager app RCE, AJP Ghostcat, default credentials, WAR file deployment, and Tomcat-specific misconfigurations

tools.caido: Caido CLI — Complete GraphQL API Guide for AIRecon
tools.code_review: Headless code review workflow for AIRecon Docker engine (CLI-only), focused on bug discovery, security risks, regressions, and test gaps.
tools.dalfox: dalfox — XSS Scanner Usage Guide for AIRecon
tools.hashcat_john: Password cracking with hashcat and John the Ripper — hash identification, attack modes, rules, wordlists, specific hash types for Windows NTLM, Linux shadow, web hashes, and Kerberos tickets
tools.impacket: Impacket toolkit — secretsdump, psexec, wmiexec, smbexec, GetUserSPNs, GetNPUsers, ntlmrelayx, ticketer, and other Windows protocol attack tools in Kali Linux
tools.metasploit: Metasploit Framework — module selection, msfconsole workflow, msfvenom payload generation, handlers, meterpreter post-exploitation, and common exploit modules for pentest/CTF
tools.nmap: nmap & naabu — Usage Guide for AIRecon
tools.source_audit: source_audit — Source Code Security Analysis Guide
tools.sqlmap: sqlmap & ghauri — Usage Guide for AIRecon
tools.tool_catalog: Tool Catalog — AIRecon Kali Linux Sandbox

vulnerabilities.2fa_bypass: 2FA/MFA bypass techniques — OTP brute force, response manipulation, race conditions, backup code abuse, CSRF bypass, SIM swapping indicators, and authentication flow manipulation
vulnerabilities.account_takeover: Account takeover methodology — password reset flaws, token predictability, OAuth misconfigurations, email verification bypass, username collision, and full ATO attack chains
vulnerabilities.api_schema_exposure: Public OpenAPI/Swagger specification exposure - detection, deep content analysis, internal hostname extraction, endpoint inventory, and attack chain building
vulnerabilities.api_testing: REST/GraphQL API security testing covering OWASP API Top 10, BOLA, mass assignment, versioning bypass, and auth flaws
vulnerabilities.authentication_jwt: JWT and OIDC security testing covering token forgery, algorithm confusion, and claim manipulation
vulnerabilities.bfla: BFLA testing for action-level authorization failures across endpoints, admin functions, and API operations
vulnerabilities.business_logic: Business logic testing for workflow bypass, state manipulation, and domain invariant violations
vulnerabilities.cors: CORS misconfiguration testing covering origin reflection, null origin bypass, trusted subdomain abuse, pre-flight bypass, and credential-bearing cross-origin requests
vulnerabilities.crlf_injection: CRLF injection — HTTP header injection, response splitting, session fixation via Set-Cookie injection, log injection, and open redirect via Location header manipulation
vulnerabilities.csrf: CSRF testing covering token bypass, SameSite cookies, CORS misconfigurations, and state-changing request abuse
vulnerabilities.csrf_advanced_bypass: Advanced CSRF bypass techniques beyond standard token removal — JSON content-type bypass, SameSite Lax exploitation, null Origin bypass, method override, parser differential attacks, and CSRF chains for maximum impact
vulnerabilities.deserialization: Insecure deserialization attacks covering Java gadget chains, PHP object injection, Python pickle, .NET, and Node.js
vulnerabilities.exploitation: Post-exploitation methodology covering shell stabilization, lateral movement, persistence, credential harvesting, and container escape
vulnerabilities.grpc: Exploitation techniques for gRPC services, targeting Protobuf serialization, HTTP/2 misconfigurations, and method enumeration.
vulnerabilities.host_header_injection: Host header injection — password reset poisoning, cache poisoning via Host header, SSRF via Host, routing bypass, and web cache deception using manipulated Host headers
vulnerabilities.http_smuggling: HTTP request smuggling via CL.TE, TE.CL, TE.TE, H2.CL, H2.TE desync attacks
vulnerabilities.idor: IDOR/BOLA testing for object-level authorization failures and cross-account data access, with automated enumeration scripts and multi-principal validation
vulnerabilities.information_disclosure: Information disclosure testing with automated scripts for .git recovery, source map extraction, JS bundle secret scanning, and DVCS artifact exploitation
vulnerabilities.insecure_file_uploads: File upload security testing covering extension bypass, content-type manipulation, and path traversal
vulnerabilities.jwt_attacks: JWT Attacks — Algorithm Confusion, None Attack, Key Confusion
vulnerabilities.kubernetes: Advanced exploitation techniques and post-exploitation for Kubernetes clusters, including container escapes, RBAC abuse, and API server attacks.
vulnerabilities.mass_assignment: Mass assignment testing with framework-specific exploitation scripts for Rails, Django, Laravel, Node.js/Express, Mongoose, and Prisma
vulnerabilities.nosql_injection: NoSQL injection testing — MongoDB operator injection, authentication bypass, blind injection, Redis command injection, and CouchDB exploitation techniques
vulnerabilities.oauth_saml: Exploitation techniques for OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect (OIDC), and SAML 2.0 implementations
vulnerabilities.open_redirect: Open redirect testing for phishing pivots, OAuth token theft, and allowlist bypass
vulnerabilities.path_traversal: Path traversal and file inclusion testing for local/remote file access and code execution
vulnerabilities.privilege_escalation: Linux and Windows privilege escalation techniques covering SUID, sudo, services, credentials, and container escape
vulnerabilities.prototype_pollution: Prototype pollution attacks covering client-side DOM XSS gadgets, server-side Node.js RCE chains, and framework-specific exploitation
vulnerabilities.race_conditions: Race condition testing for TOCTOU bugs, double-spend, and concurrent state manipulation
vulnerabilities.rce: RCE testing covering command injection, deserialization, template injection, and code evaluation
vulnerabilities.sensitive_file_pii_exposure: Detect and confirm publicly accessible sensitive documents (PDFs, DOCX, XLSX, images) containing PII via CMS REST APIs, cloud storage, directory listings, and misconfigured file servers — with PII extraction and country-specific ID pattern matching
vulnerabilities.sql_injection: SQL injection testing covering union, blind, error-based, and ORM bypass techniques
vulnerabilities.ssrf: SSRF testing for cloud metadata access, internal service discovery, and protocol smuggling
vulnerabilities.ssti: Server-Side Template Injection detection and exploitation across all major template engines
vulnerabilities.subdomain_takeover: Subdomain takeover testing for dangling DNS records and unclaimed cloud resources
vulnerabilities.supply_chain: Exploitation techniques targeting CI/CD pipelines, package ecosystems, dependency confusion, and build infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.unhandled_exception_differential: Detect unhandled exceptions in security-sensitive endpoints via differential error analysis — comparing HTTP status codes for well-formed-invalid vs. malformed inputs to identify missing exception handlers in token parsing, JSON processing, and auth validation paths
vulnerabilities.web_cache_poisoning: Web cache poisoning and cache deception attacks covering unkeyed headers, fat GET, parameter cloaking, CPDoS, and path normalization
vulnerabilities.websocket: Exploitation techniques for WebSockets including CSWSH, Smuggling, Auth bypass, and Injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.xss: XSS testing covering reflected, stored, and DOM-based vectors with CSP bypass techniques
vulnerabilities.xxe: XXE testing for external entity injection, file disclosure, and SSRF via XML parsers

guidance_messages.tracking_url_blocked: URL blocked: tracking pixel, analytics endpoint, or static asset. Browser navigation to these is not useful for vulnerability testing. Use curl or httpx for HTTP-level analysis instead.
guidance_messages.tracking_url_suggestion: Do NOT use browser_action for this URL. If you need to analyze it, use: execute(command='curl -sI {url}') for headers. Focus browser_action on interactive pages: login forms, dashboards, multi-step workflows.
guidance_messages.redirect_loop_detected: The page redirect chain exceeded 10 hops. This is usually caused by tracking pixels, ad blockers, or SSO redirect chains — not a real page.
guidance_messages.redirect_loop_suggestion: Do NOT retry the same URL. This URL has been registered as dead. If it redirected to a third-party domain (ads, analytics, SSO, or CDN), skip it and test the next endpoint.
guidance_messages.domain_dead: Unreachable. Remove from target list and proceed to next subdomain.
guidance_messages.timeout_error: Browser action timed out. Browser close requested.
guidance_messages.js_extraction_note: Auto-saved full page source. Analyze JS files for API endpoints, secrets, and vulnerabilities.
guidance_messages.console_logs_note: Auto-saved console logs. Check for errors, debug info, and leaked sensitive data.
guidance_messages.network_logs_note: Auto-saved network entries. Review for API endpoints, auth tokens, and sensitive data in responses.

recon_osint: Passive OSINT: crt.sh, Shodan dorks, WHOIS, ASN lookup, reverse-IP
Certificate transparency: crt.sh JSON API or tlsx finds subdomains brute-force misses
Wayback Machine CDX API — historical endpoints not in current sitemap
DMARC/SPF/DKIM records leak internal mail servers and infrastructure hostnames
recon_fingerprint: favicon.ico hash lookup → identify framework, find related assets on Shodan
HTTP response headers: Server, X-Powered-By, Via, Set-Cookie — full stack fingerprint
WAF/CDN fingerprint FIRST — scanning a CDN IP wastes time, find real origin
TLS certificate SAN fields contain sibling subdomains not visible via DNS
Try HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 — some endpoints respond differently under h2/h3
recon_surface: JS file analysis finds more endpoints on SPAs than directory brute-force
robots.txt + sitemap.xml list hidden paths the operator wants hidden
Check for exposed VCS or backups: /.git/, /.svn/, /backup.zip, /.env, /dump.sql
Cloud storage: enumerate target-name S3/GCS buckets
DNS zone transfer attempt — takes <1 second
Reverse IP lookup: shared hosting exposes sibling vhosts on same IP
analysis_auth: Auth testing: try token replay, JWT algorithm confusion, session fixation
OAuth/OpenID Flow: test redirect_uri manipulation, state param bypass
API auth: check for BOLA/IDOR by iterating resource IDs across sessions
Test for broken session management: concurrent sessions, logout bypass
analysis_injection: Beyond SQLi: test template injection (SSTI), LDAP injection, XPath injection
Parameter pollution: duplicate params with different values
Header injection: try injecting payloads in Host, Referer, User-Agent, X-Forwarded-For
File upload: test content-type bypass, double extension, null byte truncation
analysis_logic: Business logic: test state transitions, skip workflow steps, modify order quantities
Race conditions: parallel requests to payment/balance/quantity endpoints
Price/quantity manipulation: negative values, overflow, floating-point precision
Multi-tenancy: access another tenant's data by changing org/company ID
analysis_tech: Tech-specific: research CVEs for the EXACT versions found, not just product names
Framework-specific: check default configs, admin panels, known debug endpoints
API surface: GraphQL introspection, REST parameter pollution, GraphQL batching attacks
exploit_verify: Proof-of-concept: demonstrate actual impact, not just theoretical vulnerability
Chain findings: combine info disclosure + auth bypass for full access
Exfiltration test: extract actual data (database entries, config values)
exploit_advanced: WAF evasion: test encoding variations, parameter fragmentation, protocol switching
Authentication bypass: try JWT manipulation, cookie tampering, rate limit bypass
Privilege escalation: horizontal (same role, different user) and vertical (admin access)
